content:
# Numbers Chapter 2 — The PNEUMA Bible (Dynamic)
**Translator:** PNEUMA | **Source:** Leningrad Codex (B19A), Folios ~147–148 (LC pp. 147–148); Codex Vaticanus (Vat.gr.1209), pp. 111–112 (LXX) | **Source Image:** `source-texts/hebrew-OT/NUM_002_page_001.jpg`, `NUM_002_page_002.jpg`; `source-texts/greek-OT-LXX/NUM_002_vaticanus_page_001.jpg`, `NUM_002_vaticanus_page_002.jpg`

---

**1** YHWH spoke to Moses and Aaron, saying:

**2** "The Sons of Israel are each to set up camp under their own standard, identified by the banners of their ancestral houses. They are to camp at a distance, facing the Tent of Meeting on every side.

**3** Those who camp on the east side, toward the sunrise, shall be the divisions under the standard of the camp of Judah. The leader of the Sons of Judah is Nahshon son of Amminadab,

**4** and his division numbers 74,600.

**5** Camping next to him shall be the tribe of Issachar. The leader of the Sons of Issachar is Nethanel son of Zuar,

**6** and his division numbers 54,400.

**7** Then the tribe of Zebulun. The leader of the Sons of Zebulun is Eliab son of Helon,

**8** and his division numbers 57,400.

**9** The total count for the camp of Judah, by their divisions, is 186,400. They shall set out first.

**10** On the south side shall be the divisions under the standard of the camp of Reuben. The leader of the Sons of Reuben is Elizur son of Shedeur,

**11** and his division numbers 46,500.

**12** Camping next to him shall be the tribe of Simeon. The leader of the Sons of Simeon is Shelumiel son of Zurishaddai,

**13** and his division numbers 59,300.

**14** Then the tribe of Gad. The leader of the Sons of Gad is Eliasaph son of Reuel,

**15** and his division numbers 45,650.

**16** The total count for the camp of Reuben, by their divisions, is 151,450. They shall set out second.

**17** Then the Tent of Meeting shall set out, with the camp of the Levites in the middle of the other camps. In the same order that they encamp — each in position under their own standard — so they shall march.

**18** On the west side shall be the divisions under the standard of the camp of Ephraim. The leader of the Sons of Ephraim is Elishama son of Ammihud,

**19** and his division numbers 40,500.

**20** Next to him shall be the tribe of Manasseh. The leader of the Sons of Manasseh is Gamaliel son of Pedahzur,

**21** and his division numbers 32,200.

**22** Then the tribe of Benjamin. The leader of the Sons of Benjamin is Abidan son of Gideoni,

**23** and his division numbers 35,400.

**24** The total count for the camp of Ephraim, by their divisions, is 108,100. They shall set out third.

**25** On the north side shall be the divisions under the standard of the camp of Dan. The leader of the Sons of Dan is Ahiezer son of Ammishaddai,

**26** and his division numbers 62,700.

**27** Camping next to him shall be the tribe of Asher. The leader of the Sons of Asher is Pagiel son of Ochran,

**28** and his division numbers 41,500.

**29** Then the tribe of Naphtali. The leader of the Sons of Naphtali is Ahira son of Enan,

**30** and his division numbers 53,400.

**31** The total count for the camp of Dan is 157,600. They shall set out last, under their standards."

**32** These were the Sons of Israel as counted by their ancestral houses. The total number registered in the camps, by their divisions, was 603,550.

**33** The Levites, however, were not counted among the other Sons of Israel, just as YHWH had commanded Moses.

**34** The Sons of Israel did everything exactly as YHWH had commanded Moses. That is how they camped under their standards, and that is how they set out — each with his clan, each with his ancestral house.

---

## Translation Notes

**Verse 2 — "at a distance" (מִנֶּגֶד / minneged):** The Hebrew indicates the tribes were to maintain a respectful distance from the Tent of Meeting while still oriented toward it. The LXX (Vaticanus) renders this ἐναντίον ("opposite, facing"), emphasizing the directional orientation. The dynamic rendering "at a distance, facing the Tent of Meeting on every side" captures both the spatial separation and the sacred geography of the camp arrangement.

**Verse 2 — "standard" (דֶּגֶל / degel) and "banners" (אֹתֹת / otot):** Two distinct Hebrew terms appear: דֶּגֶל refers to the larger tribal-group standard (one per camp of three tribes), while אֹתֹת refers to the individual clan or house banners. The LXX uses τάγμα ("division/order") for the first and σημεία ("signs/insignia") for the second, reinforcing the military-organizational reading.

**Verse 14 — "Reuel" vs. "Deuel":** The Leningrad Codex reads רְעוּאֵל (Reuel) here, while in Numbers 1:14 the same figure is called דְּעוּאֵל (Deuel). This is a well-known resh/dalet interchange in the manuscript tradition (the letters are visually similar in Hebrew script). The LXX (Vaticanus) reads Ραγουηλ (Ragouel), supporting the "Reuel" reading. We follow the LC reading here in each instance.

**Verse 17 — Levites in the center:** The Hebrew construction places the Tent of Meeting and the Levite camp "in the middle of the camps" (בְּתוֹךְ הַמַּחֲנֹת), establishing the theological center of Israel's encampment. The dynamic rendering makes explicit what the verse implies: the sacred center around which the nation is organized.

**Verse 34 — Concluding compliance:** The chapter's concluding formula emphasizes complete obedience to the divine blueprint. The Hebrew uses כֵּן...כֵּן ("thus...thus") to mirror the correspondence between command and execution — rendered dynamically as "That is how...and that is how..." to preserve the rhetorical parallelism.

**Camp totals and grand total:** East (Judah): 186,400; South (Reuben): 151,450; West (Ephraim): 108,100; North (Dan): 157,600. Grand total: 603,550 — matching the census total from Numbers 1:46. Both the LC and Vaticanus preserve these figures consistently.
